Assembly: The assembly ilMelIsoc1.hap1.1 is based on 43x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was assigned by population data mapping and identification of differential coverage.

Assembly: The assembly ilLacPrae1.hap1.1 is based on 38x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 23 (9.1 - 10.5 Mbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 28 (5.9 - 8.1 Mbp) are unknown. A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 23 (11.7 - 13.4 Mbp).

Assembly: The assembly ilMelMoth8.hap1.1 is based on 41x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The autosomes in Hap1 are named by size, and the autosomes in Hap2 are named based on homology to Hap1. Chromosome 2 in Hap1 consists of two chromosomes in Hap2 (Super_2A, Super_2B).The sexchromosomes are named Z1, Z2, W1 and W2. Z1 in named based on synteny to the canonical Z, W1 is involved with an autosome fusion and the homologous autosome named Z2. The W2 is a non-fused part homologus to part of Z2.The order and orientation of the highly repetitive contigs in W1 2-4 Mb and 16.6-19.1 Mb, Hap1 Super_7 27-28.5 Mb and Hap1 Super_8 0-2.1Mb are uncertain.

Assembly: The assembly ilSarPoro1.hap1.1 is based on 74x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). A large number of telomeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences. Individual heterozygous for an inversion in chromosome 29 (from 31,800Kbp to 2,500Kbp). The exact order and orientation of the repetitive region in chromosome chromosome 28 (3,873Kpb - 4,989Kbp) is unknown.

Assembly: The assembly ilCerPisi1.1 is based on 117x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based on synteny with Orthosia gothica (GCA_949775005.1).

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Papilionoidea; Nymphalidae; Danainae; Ithomiini; Melinaeina; Melinaea
Assembly: The assembly ilMelMeno1.hap1.4 is based on 50x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The 19 autosomes in Hap1 are named after homology to the previous assembly version. There is a fusion between the W and the previously named autosome 5, the resulting fused chromosome is named W. The non-fused homologous autosome in named Z2. The canonical Z named Z. The 21 autosomes in Hap2 are named based on homology to Hap1. There are 2 heterozygous fission/fusions, the non-fused chromosomes are placed in Hap2 named 1A, 1B, 4A and 4B. Hap1 autosome 2 35.7-37.9 Mb shows high sequence identity to a part of autosome 17 (both haplotypes), likely duplication. This duplication is not present in the homologous Hap2 autosome 2A.

Assembly: This project presents the diploid genome assembly of the garden speedwell, Veronica longifolia, from a sample provided by Thomas Marcussen. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The daVerLong1.1 assembly is based on 32x PacBio HiFi and 60x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size.

Assembly: The assembly ilLacPrae1.hap2.1 is based on 38x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 23 (11.7 - 13.4 Mbp).

Assembly: The assembly ilMelMeno1.1 is based on 50x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. PAR visible at the start of Z chromosome by sequence coverage levels and Hi-C signal. Interestingly, the W chromosome has very good signal suggesting placement not only with Z as expected, but also with the left hand of chromosome 5.

Assembly: The assembly of species Botrylloides violaceus is based on 1493x long read ONT data and 1085x Arima HiC data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE,~https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). The assembly process included the following steps: ONT reads shorter than 3 kb were filtered out, thus the remaining reads for a total of about 215 Gb were assembled using Necat with polishing performed using NextPolish. Contigs were subsequently filtered with purge_dups. Scaffolding was performed by aligning HiC reads to the purged contigs using the Omni-C mapping pipeline, followed by YaHS for scaffold construction. Contamination was checked using BlobTools: 34 sequences were removed as contaminants. In addition, 2 large scaffolds (SUPER_6, SUPER_7) were classified as Mollusca. However, they were not removed because their coverage and GC content were consistent with the rest of the genome, and they showed no Hi-C contacts with other scaffolds. The scaffolds were processed through sanger_tol/curationpretext pipeline to generate the contact map, which was manually improved using PretextView. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by HiC data were named in order of size.
